The School of Self-Disappearance and Spousal Framing credential counter
workshop_pitch: You selected Seductive Deceiver plus Inherited Heir and got a villain whose favorite workplace protection is seduction. Their primary moral texture is gaslighting. Their exposure lateral is Therapeutic Capture. Their core competency stack is Coercive Reassurance. Their sidekick is a family fixer who stays because they are paid too well. Their formal training comes from The School of Self-Disappearance and Spousal Framing.
opening_image: By the time somebody names the monster correctly, the monster has already become a workflow.
dossier_summary: This build runs on proximity inside a mirrored apartment. At the story level, it turns intimacy into the delivery system for danger. The primary moral texture is gaslighting. The internal lie is simple: honesty is just timing. The exposure profile is therapeutic capture, which operationalized into coercive reassurance. The local ecosystem includes a family fixer who handles rumor and reputation ops. Before any grand plan begins, the daily proof of rot is taking your charger and acting confused about it.
public_facing_bio: Publicly, this figure presents as polished, necessary, and strangely inevitable. Privately, they are a pure engine of gaslighting shaped by therapeutic capture and confident enough to treat coercive reassurance like a normal leadership behavior. The family fixer nearby makes the whole thing feel alarmingly maintainable.
internal_memo: Internal note: continue protecting the principal from low-level reputation damage tied to taking your charger and acting confused about it. Their current strengths remain coercive reassurance, hostage empathy, selective soothing. The family fixer remains responsible for rumor and reputation ops and is currently paid too well. Do not mention the Diary Forensics Accreditation Council matter in writing.
signature_scene: The villain stages a scene inside the mirrored apartment where seduction briefly passes for order until the room notices too late that the real agenda is this: claims entitlement through lineage or prior ownership. The family fixer quietly handles rumor and reputation ops in the background, which is why the whole thing almost works. Somewhere in the room, somebody realizes too late that the sidekick is carrying the entire operation and never folds cleanly.
fake_trailer: In a world shaped by corporate satire, one seductive deceiver build will learn that the seduction, persona, or social performance is made visible enough that nobody can keep mistaking it for sincerity. Exposure: Therapeutic Capture. Core competencies: coercive reassurance, hostage empathy. Sidekick: family fixer on rumor and reputation ops. Somebody is definitely taking your charger and acting confused about it in act one and that should have been the warning.
sequel_slop: Franchise extension: somebody rebuilds the same logic with fresher branding, and the receipts get harder to kill. The petty tell survives too, because evil this scalable still finds time for taking your charger and acting confused about it. So does the sidekick ecosystem: another family fixer arrives, still paid too well, still handling rumor and reputation ops.
sample_lines: If taking your charger and acting confused about it bothers you, wait until you meet my family fixer.
My core competencies include coercive reassurance, and my family fixer handles rumor and reputation ops.
If this sounds compassionate, you are already inside the problem.
hooks: The villain is less interested in victory than in teaching the room a worse definition of normal.
Even before the grand scheme, the villain was already the kind of person who would be taking your charger and acting confused about it.
The audience trusts the build less the moment they learn even the family fixer is hanging around because they are paid too well.
